question: If the dangerousness of a school is defined by the combined total of drug, alcohol, and bomb related incidents, identify the New York City schools ranked 5th to 10th in the school years of 2012-2013, 2013-2014, and 2014-2015. For all schools in those three answers, find the top 11 schools with the most students residing in shelter in 2019-2020. Among those 11 schools, how many are in Manhattan? Return only the number in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = 'datagov/2012-2013-vadir-incidents/files/rows.txt'
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), dtype=str, low_memory=False)

incident_cols = ["Drug Possession", "Alcohol Possession", "Bomb Threat"]
for col in incident_cols:
    df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors="coerce").fillna(0)

df["School Name"] = df["School Name"].fillna("").str.strip()
df["BEDS Code"] = df["BEDS Code"].fillna("").str.strip()
df["Need/Resource Category"] = df["Need/Resource Category"].fillna("").str.strip()
filtered = df[
    df["Need/Resource Category"].eq("New York City")
    & df["School Name"].ne("")
].copy()
aggregated = filtered.groupby(["School Name", "BEDS Code"], as_index=False)[incident_cols].sum()
aggregated["dangerous_total"] = aggregated[incident_cols].sum(axis=1)
ranked = aggregated.sort_values(
    ["dangerous_total", "School Name", "BEDS Code"],
    ascending=[False, True, True],
    kind="mergesort"
).reset_index(drop=True)
answer = ", ".join(ranked.iloc[4:10]["School Name"].tolist())
1.subquestion: If the dangerousness of a school is defined by the combined total of drug, alcohol, and bomb related incidents, which New York City schools are the 5th to 10th most dangerous in New York in the school year of 2012-2013? Rank alphabetically if there is a tie and split with comma.
1.answer: Richmond Hill High School, Stuyvesant High School, High School Of Arts And Technology, Abraham Lincoln High School, Brooklyn Technical High School, Queens Metropolitan High School

4.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = 'datagov/2019-20-students-in-temporary-housing/files/rows.txt'
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), dtype=str, low_memory=False)

node_1_answer = "Richmond Hill High School, Stuyvesant High School, High School Of Arts And Technology, Abraham Lincoln High School, Brooklyn Technical High School, Queens Metropolitan High School"
node_2_answer = "Richmond Hill High School, James Madison High School, Queens Vocational And Technical High School, Art And Design High School, High School Of Graphic Communication Arts, John Bowne High School"
node_3_answer = "Tottenville High School, Bronx High School Of Science, Fort Hamilton High School, High School For Health Professions & Human Services, Long Island City High School, Benjamin N Cardozo High School"

all_schools = []
for value in [node_1_answer, node_2_answer, node_3_answer]:
    all_schools.extend([part.strip() for part in str(value).split(",") if part.strip()])
target_schools = sorted(set(all_schools))
name_to_dbn = {
    "Abraham Lincoln High School": "21K410",
    "Art And Design High School": "02M630",
    "Benjamin N Cardozo High School": "26Q415",
    "Bronx High School Of Science": "10X445",
    "Brooklyn Technical High School": "13K430",
    "Fort Hamilton High School": "20K490",
    "High School For Health Professions & Human Services": "02M420",
    "High School Of Arts And Technology": "03M494",
    "High School Of Graphic Communication Arts": "02M625",
    "James Madison High School": "22K425",
    "John Bowne High School": "25Q425",
    "Long Island City High School": "30Q450",
    "Queens Metropolitan High School": "28Q686",
    "Queens Vocational And Technical High School": "24Q600",
    "Richmond Hill High School": "27Q475",
    "Stuyvesant High School": "02M475",
    "Tottenville High School": "31R455",
}
records = pd.DataFrame([{"School Name": name, "DBN": name_to_dbn[name]} for name in target_schools if name in name_to_dbn])

df["DBN"] = df["DBN"].fillna("").str.strip()
df["# Students Residing in Shelter"] = df["# Students Residing in Shelter"].fillna("").str.strip()
df["shelter_num"] = pd.to_numeric(df["# Students Residing in Shelter"].replace({"s": pd.NA}), errors="coerce")
merged = records.merge(df[["DBN", "shelter_num"]], on="DBN", how="left")
filtered = merged[merged["shelter_num"].notna() & merged["shelter_num"].ne(0)].copy()
ranked = filtered.sort_values(["shelter_num", "School Name", "DBN"], ascending=[False, True, True], kind="mergesort").reset_index(drop=True)
answer = ", ".join(ranked["School Name"].tolist())
4.subquestion: For all schools in <hop 1 answer>, what are the top 11 schools with most students residing in shelter in the school year of 2019-2020?
4.answer: Long Island City High School, Abraham Lincoln High School, High School For Health Professions & Human Services, John Bowne High School, High School Of Arts And Technology, Benjamin N Cardozo High School, Richmond Hill High School, James Madison High School, Art And Design High School, Fort Hamilton High School, Brooklyn Technical High School

5.fact: Long Island City High School, commonly abbreviated L.I.C. or LICHS, is a public high school in Long Island City, Queens, New York City.
